name: Italian Easter Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Add milk and butter; beat for 2 minutes on medium speed.
2. Add 2 eggs and 1/2 cup flour; beat for 2 minutes on high speed. Stir in fruit, nuts, and aniseed; mix well. Stir in enough remaining flour to form a soft dough.
3. Turn dough onto a lightly floured board; knead until smooth (6-8 minutes). Place in a greased bowl; turn once. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 1 hour).
4. If desired, dye remaining eggs (leave the uncooked) and lightly rub with oil.
5. Punch dough down. Divide into two halves; roll each piece into a 24-inch rope.
6. Loosely twist ropes together; place on a greased baking sheet and form into a ring. Pinch ends together.
7. Gently split ropes and tuck eggs into openings. Cover and let rise until doubled (about 30 minutes).
8. Bake at 350 degrees for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from pan; cool on a wire rack.
